steps.message:
<uploaded_files>/app</uploaded_files>

# Incident write-up: financial-summary metrics disagree with source data at the margins

## Issue details

Several of the read-only routines that roll raw bank-feed, credit-bureau, and
accounting exports up into the structured metric blocks on the underwriting
surface are emitting figures that do not match their inputs - but only for a
narrow class of records. Every reproduction gathered so far sits on a margin of
some kind: a quantity landing exactly on a documented cut-off instead of
comfortably past it; a report handed over at the smallest column or period count
the format permits; a collection holding an even pair, so the count itself is
what decides the answer; and a field carrying one of the
reserved "nothing here" placeholder codes that is being echoed back as if it
were a genuine amount. Hand the same routines data sitting well inside the usual
ranges and the numbers come out right, which is why nothing stood out in review.

## Expected outcome

Each metric should agree with what the source record plainly says at these
margins. A value resting on a threshold is handled the way the documented rule
for that threshold states. The smallest well-formed report still yields its
per-period figures. A two-element collection is measured from its actual
contents rather than dismissed as too small. A
reserved placeholder code is discarded, never read as data. Everywhere away from
the margins the output must not move at all.

## Affected areas

The fault is contained in the deterministic, side-effect-free summarization
layer: plain arithmetic, comparisons, and roll-ups over already-parsed bank,
credit, and accounting structures. No database, network, or model component is
in the loop. Each wrong figure traces to how one boundary - or one reserved
value - is treated inside that computation, so the corrections are small and
local.

## Testing notes

The existing automated checks all pass today and must still pass afterward; they
only ever drive mid-range inputs, so they are silent about the margins described
above. Judge the fix on whether the summaries come out correct on the
exact-threshold, minimum-size, single/pair, and placeholder-code cases. Do not
touch anything under the project's `tests` tree.

Reproduce and confirm with:

    cd /app/loangen-agent && python -m pytest tests -v
I'll start by exploring the codebase structure to understand the summarization layer.

arguments.command: ls -la && echo "---" && find . -type f -name "*.py" | grep -v __pycache__ | head -100
arguments.description: List project structure and Python files

raw_arguments.command: ls -la && echo "---" && find . -type f -name "*.py" | grep -v __pycache__ | head -100

steps.message: The summarization layer works over bank (Plaid), credit bureau, and accounting (QuickBooks) data. Let me examine those tool files.

results.content:
1	"""
2	Plaid Bank Feed Service — Fetches and pre-processes bank data.
3	
4	In production: calls real Plaid APIs for account balances, transactions,
5	and uses Plaid's own categorization models for transaction classification.
6	In development: returns mock data matching the loangen-app TypeScript models.
7	
8	IMPORTANT: Transaction categorization is done by Plaid's models, NOT the LLM.
9	The LLM receives pre-categorized, aggregated financial summaries.
10	"""
11	
12	import logging
13	from typing import Any
14	
15	logger = logging.getLogger("loangen-plaid")
16	
17	MOCK_PLAID_DATA: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {
18	    "qb-001": {
19	        "company_id": "qb-001",
20	        "linked_date": "2024-06-15",
21	        "last_refresh": "2025-01-15",
22	        "accounts": [
23	            {
24	                "account_id": "acc-001-checking",
25	                "institution_name": "Chase",
26	                "account_name": "Business Checking",
27	                "account_type": "checking",
28	                "mask": "4521",
29	                "current_balance": 84500,
30	                "available_balance": 82000,
31	            },
32	            {
33	                "account_id": "acc-001-savings",
34	                "institution_name": "Chase",

36	                "account_type": "savings",

40	            },
41	        ],
42	        "monthly_snapshots": [
43	            {"month": "2024-07", "total_deposits": 142000, "total_withdrawals": 118000, "ending_balance": 78500, "average_daily_balance": 72000, "number_of_deposits": 45, "number_of_withdrawals": 62, "largest_deposit": 28000, "largest_withdrawal": 15000, "negative_days": 0, "nsf_overdraft_count": 0},

48	            {"month": "2024-12", "total_deposits": 170000, "total_withdrawals": 145000, "ending_balance": 194500, "average_daily_balance": 98000, "number_of_deposits": 52, "number_of_withdrawals": 72, "largest_deposit": 42000, "largest_withdrawal": 25000, "negative_days": 0, "nsf_overdraft_count": 0},

205	class PlaidBankService:
206	    """
207	    Fetches bank feed data. In dev mode, returns mock data.
208	    In production, calls real Plaid APIs.
209	
210	    Transaction categorization is done by Plaid's categorization models,
211	    NOT the LLM. The LLM only receives pre-categorized aggregated summaries.
212	    """
213	
214	    async def get_bank_data(self, company_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
215	        data = MOCK_PLAID_DATA.get(company_id)
216	        if not data:
217	            logger.warning(f"No Plaid data for company {company_id}")
218	            return None
219	        return data
220	
221	    async def get_cash_flow_score(self, company_id: str) -> int | None:
222	        data = MOCK_PLAID_DATA.get(company_id)
223	        return data.get("cash_flow_score") if data else None
224	
225	    async def get_monthly_trend(self, company_id: str) -> list[dict] | None:
226	        data = MOCK_PLAID_DATA.get(company_id)
227	        return data.get("monthly_snapshots") if data else None

Plaid Bank Feed Service — Fetches and pre-processes bank data.

In production: calls real Plaid APIs for account balances, transactions,
and uses Plaid's own categorization models for transaction classification.
In development: returns mock data matching the loangen-app TypeScript models.

IMPORTANT: Transaction categorization is done by Plaid's models, NOT the LLM.
The LLM receives pre-categorized, aggregated financial summaries.
"""

class PlaidBankService:
    """
    Fetches bank feed data. In dev mode, returns mock data.
    In production, calls real Plaid APIs.

    Transaction categorization is done by Plaid's categorization models,
    NOT the LLM. The LLM only receives pre-categorized aggregated summaries.
    """

    async def get_bank_data(self, company_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
        data = MOCK_PLAID_DATA.get(company_id)
        if not data:
            logger.warning(f"No Plaid data for company {company_id}")
            return None
        return data

    async def get_cash_flow_score(self, company_id: str) -> int | None:
        data = MOCK_PLAID_DATA.get(company_id)
        return data.get("cash_flow_score") if data else None

    async def get_monthly_trend(self, company_id: str) -> list[dict] | None:
        data = MOCK_PLAID_DATA.get(company_id)
        return data.get("monthly_snapshots") if data else None

205	class PlaidBankService:
206	    """
207	    Fetches bank feed data. In dev mode, returns mock data.
208	    In production, calls real Plaid APIs.
209	
210	    Transaction categorization is done by Plaid's categorization models,
211	    NOT the LLM. The LLM only receives pre-categorized aggregated summaries.
212	    """
213	
214	    async def get_bank_data(self, company_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
215	        data = MOCK_PLAID_DATA.get(company_id)
216	        if not data:
217	            logger.warning(f"No Plaid data for company {company_id}")
218	            return None
219	        return data
220	
221	    async def get_cash_flow_score(self, company_id: str) -> int | None:
222	        data = MOCK_PLAID_DATA.get(company_id)
223	        return data.get("cash_flow_score") if data else None
224	
225	    async def get_monthly_trend(self, company_id: str) -> list[dict] | None:
226	        data = MOCK_PLAID_DATA.get(company_id)
227	        return data.get("monthly_snapshots") if data else None
228

QuickBooks Service — Fetches and pre-processes QuickBooks financial data.

In production: calls the Next.js data API or QuickBooks API directly.
In development: returns mock data matching the loangen-app TypeScript models.

Financial ratios (current ratio, D/E, profit margin) are pre-computed HERE,
not by the LLM. The LLM receives verified numbers as context.

class QuickBooksService:
    """
    Fetches QuickBooks data. In dev mode, returns mock data.
    In production, calls the Next.js API or QuickBooks API directly.

    All financial ratios are pre-computed here, not by the LLM.
    """

    async def get_company(self, company_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
        company = MOCK_COMPANIES.get(company_id)
        if not company:
            logger.warning(f"No QuickBooks company for {company_id}")
        return company

    async def get_financials(self, company_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
        financials = MOCK_FINANCIALS.get(company_id)
        if not financials:
            logger.warning(f"No QuickBooks financials for {company_id}")
        return financials

    async def get_readiness_score(self, company_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
        return MOCK_READINESS_SCORES.get(company_id)

    async def list_companies(self) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
        return list(MOCK_COMPANIES.values())

216	class QuickBooksService:
217	    """
218	    Fetches QuickBooks data. In dev mode, returns mock data.
219	    In production, calls the Next.js API or QuickBooks API directly.
220	
221	    All financial ratios are pre-computed here, not by the LLM.
222	    """
223	
224	    async def get_company(self, company_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
225	        company = MOCK_COMPANIES.get(company_id)
226	        if not company:
227	            logger.warning(f"No QuickBooks company for {company_id}")
228	        return company
229	
230	    async def get_financials(self, company_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
231	        financials = MOCK_FINANCIALS.get(company_id)
232	        if not financials:
233	            logger.warning(f"No QuickBooks financials for {company_id}")
234	        return financials
235	
236	    async def get_readiness_score(self, company_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
237	        return MOCK_READINESS_SCORES.get(company_id)
238	
239	    async def list_companies(self) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
240	        return list(MOCK_COMPANIES.values())

214	class CreditBureauService:
215	    """
216	    Fetches credit bureau data. In dev mode, returns mock data.
217	    In production, calls real bureau APIs.
218	
219	    Data is returned as pre-processed dicts — the LLM receives
220	    these structured summaries, never raw bureau XML/JSON.
221	    """
222	
223	    async def get_reports(self, company_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
224	        personal = MOCK_PERSONAL_CREDIT.get(company_id)
225	        business = MOCK_BUSINESS_CREDIT.get(company_id)
226	
227	        if not personal and not business:
228	            logger.warning(f"No credit data for company {company_id}")
229	            return None
230	
231	        return {
232	            "personal": personal,
233	            "business": business,
234	        }
235	
236	    async def get_fico_score(self, company_id: str) -> int | None:
237	        personal = MOCK_PERSONAL_CREDIT.get(company_id)
238	        return personal["fico_score"] if personal else None

"""
Credit Bureau Service — Fetches and pre-processes credit data.

In production: calls real credit bureau APIs (Experian, Equifax, TransUnion).
In development: returns mock data matching the loangen-app TypeScript models.

The credit data is PRE-PROCESSED here before being sent to the LLM.
The LLM never sees raw bureau responses — only structured summaries.
"""

from typing import Any

logger = logging.getLogger("loangen-credit")

class CreditBureauService:
    """
    Fetches credit bureau data. In dev mode, returns mock data.
    In production, calls real bureau APIs.

    Data is returned as pre-processed dicts — the LLM receives
    these structured summaries, never raw bureau XML/JSON.
    """

    async def get_reports(self, company_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
        personal = MOCK_PERSONAL_CREDIT.get(company_id)
        business = MOCK_BUSINESS_CREDIT.get(company_id)

        if not personal and not business:
            logger.warning(f"No credit data for company {company_id}")
            return None

        return {
            "personal": personal,
            "business": business,
        }

    async def get_fico_score(self, company_id: str) -> int | None:
        personal = MOCK_PERSONAL_CREDIT.get(company_id)
        return personal["fico_score"] if personal else None

214	class CreditBureauService:
215	    """
216	    Fetches credit bureau data. In dev mode, returns mock data.
217	    In production, calls real bureau APIs.
218	
219	    Data is returned as pre-processed dicts — the LLM receives
220	    these structured summaries, never raw bureau XML/JSON.
221	    """
222	
223	    async def get_reports(self, company_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
224	        personal = MOCK_PERSONAL_CREDIT.get(company_id)
225	        business = MOCK_BUSINESS_CREDIT.get(company_id)
226	
227	        if not personal and not business:
228	            logger.warning(f"No credit data for company {company_id}")
229	            return None
230	
231	        return {
232	            "personal": personal,
233	            "business": business,
234	        }
235	
236	    async def get_fico_score(self, company_id: str) -> int | None:
237	        personal = MOCK_PERSONAL_CREDIT.get(company_id)
238	        return personal["fico_score"] if personal else None

1	"""
2	Qualification Engine — Deterministic loan qualification logic.
3	
4	This is the AUTHORITATIVE qualification engine. It computes qualification
5	results against a list of loan products (from DB). Products are passed in;
6	no hardcoded product list.
7	"""
8	
9	import logging
10	from datetime import datetime
11	from typing import Any
12	
13	logger = logging.getLogger("loangen-qualification")
14	
15	
16	def _safe_num(v: Any) -> float:
17	    """Coerce to float for use in sums; None or invalid -> 0."""
18	    if v is None:
19	        return 0.0
20	    try:
21	        return float(v)
22	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
23	        return 0.0
24	
25	
26	def compute_dscr(
27	    net_income: float,
28	    monthly_debt_payments: float,
29	) -> float:
30	    """Deterministic DSCR computation. Used as ground truth, not the LLM."""
31	    monthly_income = net_income / 12
32	    if monthly_debt_payments <= 0:
33	        return 99.0
34	    return round(monthly_income / monthly_debt_payments, 2)
35	
36	
37	class QualificationEngine:
38	    """
39	    Deterministic qualification logic. Computes exact pass/fail against
40	    each loan product's criteria and identifies specific gaps.
41	
42	    The LLM receives these results as structured context and reasons
43	    over them — it does NOT perform the qualification math.
44	    """
45	
46	    def qualify(
47	        self,
48	        context: dict[str, Any],
49	        products: list[dict[str, Any]],
50	        desired_loan_type: str = "general",
51	        desired_amount: float = 0,
52	    ) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:
53	        """
54	        Qualify the SMB against the given loan products. Returns a dict mapping
55	        product id -> qualification result (qualifies, gaps, match_score, etc.).
56	        products: list of dicts with id, name, category, min_amount, max_amount,
57	                  rate_range, term_months, criteria (min_credit_score, min_dscr, ...).
58	        """
59	        results: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
60	
61	        company = context.get("company") or {}
62	        financials = context.get("financials") or {}
63	        personal_credit = context.get("credit_personal") or {}
64	        bank = context.get("bank_data") or {}
65	        cash_flow = bank.get("cash_flow_analysis") or {}
66	
67	        fico = personal_credit.get("fico_score", 0)
68	        revenue = financials.get("revenue", 0)
69	        _now_year = datetime.now().year
70	        try:
71	            year_est = company.get("year_established")
72	            year_est = _now_year if year_est is None else int(year_est)
73	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
74	            year_est = _now_year
75	        years_in_biz = _now_year - year_est
76	        d_to_e = financials.get("debt_to_equity", 0)
77	        net_income = financials.get("net_income", 0)
78	
79	        monthly_debt = sum(
80	            _safe_num(t.get("monthly_payment"))
81	            for t in personal_credit.get("tradelines", [])
82	            if t.get("status") != "closed"
83	        )
84	        property_dscr = financials.get("property_dscr") or financials.get("dscr")
85	        if property_dscr is not None and float(property_dscr) > 0:
86	            dscr = round(float(property_dscr), 2)
87	        else:
88	            dscr = compute_dscr(net_income, monthly_debt)
89	
90	        for product in products:
91	            crit = product.get("criteria") or {}
92	            pid = str(product.get("id", ""))
93	            if not pid:
94	                continue
95	            gaps: list[str] = []
96	            passed = True
97	
98	            if fico < crit.get("min_credit_score", 0):
99	                passed = False
100	                gaps.append(
101	                    f"Credit score {fico} below minimum {crit['min_credit_score']} "
102	                    f"(gap: {crit['min_credit_score'] - fico} points)"
103	                )
104	
105	            if revenue < crit.get("min_annual_revenue", 0):
106	                passed = False
107	                gaps.append(
108	                    f"Annual revenue ${revenue:,.0f} below minimum ${crit['min_annual_revenue']:,.0f} "
109	                    f"(gap: ${crit['min_annual_revenue'] - revenue:,.0f})"
110	                )
111	
112	            if years_in_biz < crit.get("min_years_in_business", 0):
113	                passed = False
114	                gaps.append(
115	                    f"Time in business {years_in_biz}yr below minimum {crit['min_years_in_business']}yr"
116	                )
117	
118	            if crit.get("min_dscr") is not None and dscr < crit["min_dscr"]:
119	                passed = False
120	                gaps.append(
121	                    f"DSCR {dscr} below minimum {crit['min_dscr']} "
122	                    f"(gap: {crit['min_dscr'] - dscr:.2f})"
123	                )
124	
125	            if crit.get("max_debt_to_equity") is not None and d_to_e > crit["max_debt_to_equity"]:
126	                passed = False
127	                gaps.append(
128	                    f"Debt-to-equity {d_to_e:.2f} exceeds maximum {crit['max_debt_to_equity']}"
129	                )
130	
131	            amount_ok = True
132	            min_amt = product.get("min_amount", 0)
133	            max_amt = product.get("max_amount", 0)
134	            if desired_amount > 0:
135	                if desired_amount < min_amt or desired_amount > max_amt:
136	                    amount_ok = False
137	                    gaps.append(
138	                        f"Requested ${desired_amount:,.0f} outside range "
139	                        f"${min_amt:,.0f}-${max_amt:,.0f}"
140	                    )
141	
142	            max_qualified = min(revenue * 0.30, max_amt) if passed else 0
143	
144	            score = 100
145	            if not passed:
146	                score = max(0, 100 - len(gaps) * 20)
147	
148	            results[pid] = {
149	                "product_id": pid,
150	                "product_name": product.get("name", ""),
151	                "qualifies": passed and amount_ok,
152	                "gaps": gaps,
153	                "match_score": score,
154	                "max_qualified_amount": max_qualified,
155	                "rate_range": product.get("rate_range") or {"min": 0, "max": 0},
156	                "term_range": product.get("term_months") or {"min": 0, "max": 0},
157	                "dscr": dscr,
158	            }
159	
160	        return results
161	
162	    def match_lenders(
163	        self,
164	        context: dict[str, Any],
165	        desired_loan_type: str = "general",
166	    ) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
167	        """
168	        Match against real lenders. Returns a sorted list of lender matches.
169	        This is deterministic logic, not LLM reasoning.
170	        """
171	        company = context.get("company") or {}
172	        financials = context.get("financials") or {}
173	        personal_credit = context.get("credit_personal") or {}
174	
175	        fico = personal_credit.get("fico_score", 0)
176	        revenue = financials.get("revenue", 0)
177	        _now_year = datetime.now().year
178	        try:
179	            year_est = company.get("year_established")
180	            year_est = _now_year if year_est is None else int(year_est)
181	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
182	            year_est = _now_year
183	        months_in_biz = (_now_year - year_est) * 12
184	
185	        net_income = financials.get("net_income", 0)
186	        monthly_debt = sum(
187	            _safe_num(t.get("monthly_payment"))
188	            for t in personal_credit.get("tradelines", [])
189	            if t.get("status") != "closed"
190	        )
191	        dscr = compute_dscr(net_income, monthly_debt)
192	        d_to_e = financials.get("debt_to_equity", 0)
193	
194	        matches = []
195	        for lender in REAL_LENDERS:
196	            crit = lender["criteria"]
197	            gaps: list[str] = []
198	            reasons: list[str] = []
199	            score = 0
200	
201	            if fico >= crit["min_credit_score"]:
202	                score += 25
203	                reasons.append(f"Credit score {fico} meets {crit['min_credit_score']} minimum")
204	            else:
205	                gaps.append(f"Credit score {fico} < {crit['min_credit_score']}")
206	
207	            if revenue >= crit["min_annual_revenue"]:
208	                score += 25
209	                reasons.append(f"Revenue ${revenue:,.0f} meets minimum")
210	            else:
211	                gaps.append(f"Revenue ${revenue:,.0f} < ${crit['min_annual_revenue']:,.0f}")
212	
213	            if months_in_biz >= crit["min_time_in_business_months"]:
214	                score += 20
215	                reasons.append(f"{months_in_biz // 12}yr in business meets requirement")
216	            else:
217	                gaps.append(f"Time in business {months_in_biz}mo < {crit['min_time_in_business_months']}mo")
218	
219	            if crit.get("min_dscr") and crit["min_dscr"] > 0:
220	                if dscr >= crit["min_dscr"]:
221	                    score += 15
222	                    reasons.append(f"DSCR {dscr} meets {crit['min_dscr']} minimum")
223	                else:
224	                    gaps.append(f"DSCR {dscr} < {crit['min_dscr']}")
225	            else:
226	                score += 15
227	
228	            if crit.get("max_debt_to_equity") and crit["max_debt_to_equity"] > 0:
229	                if d_to_e <= crit["max_debt_to_equity"]:
230	                    score += 15
231	                else:
232	                    gaps.append(f"D/E {d_to_e:.2f} > max {crit['max_debt_to_equity']}")
233	            else:
234	                score += 15
235	
236	            matches.append({
237	                "lender_id": lender["id"],
238	                "name": lender["name"],
239	                "qualifies": len(gaps) == 0,
240	                "match_score": score,
241	                "gaps": gaps,
242	                "match_reasons": reasons,
243	                "rate_range": f"{lender['rate_range']['min']}-{lender['rate_range']['max']}%",
244	                "loan_range": lender["loan_range"],
245	                "funding_speed": lender["funding_speed"],
246	                "api_status": lender["api_integration"]["status"],
247	            })
248	
249	        matches.sort(key=lambda m: (-int(m["qualifies"]), -m["match_score"]))
250	        return matches
251	
252	    def detect_savings_opportunities(self, context: dict[str, Any]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
253	        """
254	        Detect cross-sell / savings opportunities from financial data.
255	        Uses deterministic rules and benchmarks, not LLM reasoning.
256	        """
257	        financials = context.get("financials") or {}
258	        bank = context.get("bank_data") or {}
259	        cash_flow = bank.get("cash_flow_analysis") or {}
260	        revenue = financials.get("revenue", 0)
261	        expenses = financials.get("operating_expenses", 0)
262	
263	        opportunities = []
264	
265	        if revenue > 500000:
266	            estimated_processing = revenue * 0.028
267	            benchmark = revenue * 0.018
268	            if estimated_processing > benchmark + 2000:
269	                opportunities.append({
270	                    "type": "Payment Processing",
271	                    "description": "Your transaction volume suggests you may be overpaying on card processing fees",
272	                    "estimated_savings": estimated_processing - benchmark,
273	                    "current_spend": estimated_processing,
274	                    "benchmark_spend": benchmark,
275	                })
276	
277	        if expenses > 0:
278	            insurance_estimate = expenses * 0.08
279	            benchmark_insurance = expenses * 0.05
280	            if insurance_estimate > benchmark_insurance + 3000:
281	                opportunities.append({
282	                    "type": "Business Insurance",
283	                    "description": "Your insurance costs appear higher than industry average",
284	                    "estimated_savings": insurance_estimate - benchmark_insurance,
285	                    "current_spend": insurance_estimate,
286	                    "benchmark_spend": benchmark_insurance,
287	                })
288	
289	        utilization = cash_flow.get("income_volatility", 0)
290	        if utilization > 20:
291	            opportunities.append({
292	                "type": "Cash Flow Management",
293	                "description": "High revenue volatility detected — a line of credit could smooth cash flow gaps",
294	                "estimated_savings": 0,
295	                "current_spend": 0,
296	                "benchmark_spend": 0,
297	            })
298	
299	        return opportunities
300

"""
Qualification Engine — Deterministic loan qualification logic.

This is the AUTHORITATIVE qualification engine. It computes qualification
results against a list of loan products (from DB). Products are passed in;
no hardcoded product list.
"""

import logging
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Any

logger = logging.getLogger("loangen-qualification")


def _safe_num(v: Any) -> float:
    """Coerce to float for use in sums; None or invalid -> 0."""
    if v is None:
        return 0.0
    try:
        return float(v)
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        return 0.0


def compute_dscr(
    net_income: float,
    monthly_debt_payments: float,
) -> float:
    """Deterministic DSCR computation. Used as ground truth, not the LLM."""
    monthly_income = net_income / 12
    if monthly_debt_payments <= 0:
        return 99.0
    return round(monthly_income / monthly_debt_payments, 2)


class QualificationEngine:
    """
    Deterministic qualification logic. Computes exact pass/fail against
    each loan product's criteria and identifies specific gaps.

    The LLM receives these results as structured context and reasons
    over them — it does NOT perform the qualification math.
    """

    def qualify(
        self,
        context: dict[str, Any],
        products: list[dict[str, Any]],
        desired_loan_type: str = "general",
        desired_amount: float = 0,
    ) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:
        """
        Qualify the SMB against the given loan products. Returns a dict mapping
        product id -> qualification result (qualifies, gaps, match_score, etc.).
        products: list of dicts with id, name, category, min_amount, max_amount,
                  rate_range, term_months, criteria (min_credit_score, min_dscr, ...).
        """
        results: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}

        company = context.get("company") or {}
        financials = context.get("financials") or {}
        personal_credit = context.get("credit_personal") or {}
        bank = context.get("bank_data") or {}
        cash_flow = bank.get("cash_flow_analysis") or {}

        fico = personal_credit.get("fico_score", 0)
        revenue = financials.get("revenue", 0)
        _now_year = datetime.now().year
        try:
            year_est = company.get("year_established")
            year_est = _now_year if year_est is None else int(year_est)
        except (TypeError, ValueError):
            year_est = _now_year
        years_in_biz = _now_year - year_est
        d_to_e = financials.get("debt_to_equity", 0)
        net_income = financials.get("net_income", 0)

        monthly_debt = sum(
            _safe_num(t.get("monthly_payment"))
            for t in personal_credit.get("tradelines", [])
            if t.get("status") != "closed"
        )
        property_dscr = financials.get("property_dscr") or financials.get("dscr")
        if property_dscr is not None and float(property_dscr) > 0:
            dscr = round(float(property_dscr), 2)
        else:
            dscr = compute_dscr(net_income, monthly_debt)

        for product in products:
            crit = product.get("criteria") or {}
            pid = str(product.get("id", ""))
            if not pid:
                continue
            gaps: list[str] = []
            passed = True

            if fico < crit.get("min_credit_score", 0):
                passed = False
                gaps.append(
                    f"Credit score {fico} below minimum {crit['min_credit_score']} "
                    f"(gap: {crit['min_credit_score'] - fico} points)"
                )

            if revenue < crit.get("min_annual_revenue", 0):
                passed = False
                gaps.append(
                    f"Annual revenue ${revenue:,.0f} below minimum ${crit['min_annual_revenue']:,.0f} "
                    f"(gap: ${crit['min_annual_revenue'] - revenue:,.0f})"
                )

            if years_in_biz < crit.get("min_years_in_business", 0):
                passed = False
                gaps.append(
                    f"Time in business {years_in_biz}yr below minimum {crit['min_years_in_business']}yr"
                )

            if crit.get("min_dscr") is not None and dscr < crit["min_dscr"]:
                passed = False
                gaps.append(
                    f"DSCR {dscr} below minimum {crit['min_dscr']} "
                    f"(gap: {crit['min_dscr'] - dscr:.2f})"
                )

            if crit.get("max_debt_to_equity") is not None and d_to_e > crit["max_debt_to_equity"]:
                passed = False
                gaps.append(
                    f"Debt-to-equity {d_to_e:.2f} exceeds maximum {crit['max_debt_to_equity']}"
                )

            amount_ok = True
            min_amt = product.get("min_amount", 0)
            max_amt = product.get("max_amount", 0)
            if desired_amount > 0:
                if desired_amount < min_amt or desired_amount > max_amt:
                    amount_ok = False
                    gaps.append(
                        f"Requested ${desired_amount:,.0f} outside range "
                        f"${min_amt:,.0f}-${max_amt:,.0f}"
                    )

            max_qualified = min(revenue * 0.30, max_amt) if passed else 0

            score = 100
            if not passed:
                score = max(0, 100 - len(gaps) * 20)

            results[pid] = {
                "product_id": pid,
                "product_name": product.get("name", ""),
                "qualifies": passed and amount_ok,
                "gaps": gaps,
                "match_score": score,
                "max_qualified_amount": max_qualified,
                "rate_range": product.get("rate_range") or {"min": 0, "max": 0},
                "term_range": product.get("term_months") or {"min": 0, "max": 0},
                "dscr": dscr,
            }

        return results

    def match_lenders(
        self,
        context: dict[str, Any],
        desired_loan_type: str = "general",
    ) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
        """
        Match against real lenders. Returns a sorted list of lender matches.
        This is deterministic logic, not LLM reasoning.
        """
        company = context.get("company") or {}
        financials = context.get("financials") or {}
        personal_credit = context.get("credit_personal") or {}

        fico = personal_credit.get("fico_score", 0)
        revenue = financials.get("revenue", 0)
        _now_year = datetime.now().year
        try:
            year_est = company.get("year_established")
            year_est = _now_year if year_est is None else int(year_est)
        except (TypeError, ValueError):
            year_est = _now_year
        months_in_biz = (_now_year - year_est) * 12

        net_income = financials.get("net_income", 0)
        monthly_debt = sum(
            _safe_num(t.get("monthly_payment"))
            for t in personal_credit.get("tradelines", [])
            if t.get("status") != "closed"
        )
        dscr = compute_dscr(net_income, monthly_debt)
        d_to_e = financials.get("debt_to_equity", 0)

        matches = []
        for lender in REAL_LENDERS:
            crit = lender["criteria"]
            gaps: list[str] = []
            reasons: list[str] = []
            score = 0

            if fico >= crit["min_credit_score"]:
                score += 25
                reasons.append(f"Credit score {fico} meets {crit['min_credit_score']} minimum")
            else:
                gaps.append(f"Credit score {fico} < {crit['min_credit_score']}")

            if revenue >= crit["min_annual_revenue"]:
                score += 25
                reasons.append(f"Revenue ${revenue:,.0f} meets minimum")
            else:
                gaps.append(f"Revenue ${revenue:,.0f} < ${crit['min_annual_revenue']:,.0f}")

            if months_in_biz >= crit["min_time_in_business_months"]:
                score += 20
                reasons.append(f"{months_in_biz // 12}yr in business meets requirement")
            else:
                gaps.append(f"Time in business {months_in_biz}mo < {crit['min_time_in_business_months']}mo")

            if crit.get("min_dscr") and crit["min_dscr"] > 0:
                if dscr >= crit["min_dscr"]:
                    score += 15
                    reasons.append(f"DSCR {dscr} meets {crit['min_dscr']} minimum")
                else:
                    gaps.append(f"DSCR {dscr} < {crit['min_dscr']}")
            else:
                score += 15

            if crit.get("max_debt_to_equity") and crit["max_debt_to_equity"] > 0:
                if d_to_e <= crit["max_debt_to_equity"]:
                    score += 15
                else:
                    gaps.append(f"D/E {d_to_e:.2f} > max {crit['max_debt_to_equity']}")
            else:
                score += 15

            matches.append({
                "lender_id": lender["id"],
                "name": lender["name"],
                "qualifies": len(gaps) == 0,
                "match_score": score,
                "gaps": gaps,
                "match_reasons": reasons,
                "rate_range": f"{lender['rate_range']['min']}-{lender['rate_range']['max']}%",
                "loan_range": lender["loan_range"],
                "funding_speed": lender["funding_speed"],
                "api_status": lender["api_integration"]["status"],
            })

        matches.sort(key=lambda m: (-int(m["qualifies"]), -m["match_score"]))
        return matches

    def detect_savings_opportunities(self, context: dict[str, Any]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
        """
        Detect cross-sell / savings opportunities from financial data.
        Uses deterministic rules and benchmarks, not LLM reasoning.
        """
        financials = context.get("financials") or {}
        bank = context.get("bank_data") or {}
        cash_flow = bank.get("cash_flow_analysis") or {}
        revenue = financials.get("revenue", 0)
        expenses = financials.get("operating_expenses", 0)

        opportunities = []

        if revenue > 500000:
            estimated_processing = revenue * 0.028
            benchmark = revenue * 0.018
            if estimated_processing > benchmark + 2000:
                opportunities.append({
                    "type": "Payment Processing",
                    "description": "Your transaction volume suggests you may be overpaying on card processing fees",
                    "estimated_savings": estimated_processing - benchmark,
                    "current_spend": estimated_processing,
                    "benchmark_spend": benchmark,
                })

        if expenses > 0:
            insurance_estimate = expenses * 0.08
            benchmark_insurance = expenses * 0.05
            if insurance_estimate > benchmark_insurance + 3000:
                opportunities.append({
                    "type": "Business Insurance",
                    "description": "Your insurance costs appear higher than industry average",
                    "estimated_savings": insurance_estimate - benchmark_insurance,
                    "current_spend": insurance_estimate,
                    "benchmark_spend": benchmark_insurance,
                })

        utilization = cash_flow.get("income_volatility", 0)
        if utilization > 20:
            opportunities.append({
                "type": "Cash Flow Management",
                "description": "High revenue volatility detected — a line of credit could smooth cash flow gaps",
                "estimated_savings": 0,
                "current_spend": 0,
                "benchmark_spend": 0,
            })

        return opportunities

1	"""
2	Qualification Engine — Deterministic loan qualification logic.
3	
4	This is the AUTHORITATIVE qualification engine. It computes qualification
5	results against a list of loan products (from DB). Products are passed in;
6	no hardcoded product list.
7	"""
8	
9	import logging
10	from datetime import datetime
11	from typing import Any
12	
13	logger = logging.getLogger("loangen-qualification")
14	
15	
16	def _safe_num(v: Any) -> float:
17	    """Coerce to float for use in sums; None or invalid -> 0."""
18	    if v is None:
19	        return 0.0
20	    try:
21	        return float(v)
22	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
23	        return 0.0
24	
25	
26	def compute_dscr(
27	    net_income: float,
28	    monthly_debt_payments: float,
29	) -> float:
30	    """Deterministic DSCR computation. Used as ground truth, not the LLM."""
31	    monthly_income = net_income / 12
32	    if monthly_debt_payments <= 0:
33	        return 99.0
34	    return round(monthly_income / monthly_debt_payments, 2)
35	
36	
37	class QualificationEngine:
38	    """
39	    Deterministic qualification logic. Computes exact pass/fail against
40	    each loan product's criteria and identifies specific gaps.
41	
42	    The LLM receives these results as structured context and reasons
43	    over them — it does NOT perform the qualification math.
44	    """
45	
46	    def qualify(
47	        self,
48	        context: dict[str, Any],
49	        products: list[dict[str, Any]],
50	        desired_loan_type: str = "general",
51	        desired_amount: float = 0,
52	    ) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:
53	        """
54	        Qualify the SMB against the given loan products. Returns a dict mapping
55	        product id -> qualification result (qualifies, gaps, match_score, etc.).
56	        products: list of dicts with id, name, category, min_amount, max_amount,
57	                  rate_range, term_months, criteria (min_credit_score, min_dscr, ...).
58	        """
59	        results: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
60	
61	        company = context.get("company") or {}
62	        financials = context.get("financials") or {}
63	        personal_credit = context.get("credit_personal") or {}
64	        bank = context.get("bank_data") or {}
65	        cash_flow = bank.get("cash_flow_analysis") or {}
66	
67	        fico = personal_credit.get("fico_score", 0)
68	        revenue = financials.get("revenue", 0)
69	        _now_year = datetime.now().year
70	        try:
71	            year_est = company.get("year_established")
72	            year_est = _now_year if year_est is None else int(year_est)
73	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
74	            year_est = _now_year
75	        years_in_biz = _now_year - year_est
76	        d_to_e = financials.get("debt_to_equity", 0)
77	        net_income = financials.get("net_income", 0)
78	
79	        monthly_debt = sum(
80	            _safe_num(t.get("monthly_payment"))
81	            for t in personal_credit.get("tradelines", [])
82	            if t.get("status") != "closed"
83	        )
84	        property_dscr = financials.get("property_dscr") or financials.get("dscr")
85	        if property_dscr is not None and float(property_dscr) > 0:
86	            dscr = round(float(property_dscr), 2)
87	        else:
88	            dscr = compute_dscr(net_income, monthly_debt)
89	
90	        for product in products:
91	            crit = product.get("criteria") or {}
92	            pid = str(product.get("id", ""))
93	            if not pid:
94	                continue
95	            gaps: list[str] = []
96	            passed = True
97	
98	            if fico < crit.get("min_credit_score", 0):
99	                passed = False
100	                gaps.append(
101	                    f"Credit score {fico} below minimum {crit['min_credit_score']} "
102	                    f"(gap: {crit['min_credit_score'] - fico} points)"
103	                )
104	
105	            if revenue < crit.get("min_annual_revenue", 0):
106	                passed = False
107	                gaps.append(
108	                    f"Annual revenue ${revenue:,.0f} below minimum ${crit['min_annual_revenue']:,.0f} "
109	                    f"(gap: ${crit['min_annual_revenue'] - revenue:,.0f})"
110	                )
111	
112	            if years_in_biz < crit.get("min_years_in_business", 0):
113	                passed = False
114	                gaps.append(
115	                    f"Time in business {years_in_biz}yr below minimum {crit['min_years_in_business']}yr"
116	                )
117	
118	            if crit.get("min_dscr") is not None and dscr < crit["min_dscr"]:
119	                passed = False
120	                gaps.append(
121	                    f"DSCR {dscr} below minimum {crit['min_dscr']} "
122	                    f"(gap: {crit['min_dscr'] - dscr:.2f})"
123	                )
124	
125	            if crit.get("max_debt_to_equity") is not None and d_to_e > crit["max_debt_to_equity"]:
126	                passed = False
127	                gaps.append(
128	                    f"Debt-to-equity {d_to_e:.2f} exceeds maximum {crit['max_debt_to_equity']}"
129	                )
130	
131	            amount_ok = True
132	            min_amt = product.get("min_amount", 0)
133	            max_amt = product.get("max_amount", 0)
134	            if desired_amount > 0:
135	                if desired_amount < min_amt or desired_amount > max_amt:
136	                    amount_ok = False
137	                    gaps.append(
138	                        f"Requested ${desired_amount:,.0f} outside range "
139	                        f"${min_amt:,.0f}-${max_amt:,.0f}"
140	                    )
141	
142	            max_qualified = min(revenue * 0.30, max_amt) if passed else 0
143	
144	            score = 100
145	            if not passed:
146	                score = max(0, 100 - len(gaps) * 20)
147	
148	            results[pid] = {
149	                "product_id": pid,
150	                "product_name": product.get("name", ""),
151	                "qualifies": passed and amount_ok,
152	                "gaps": gaps,
153	                "match_score": score,
154	                "max_qualified_amount": max_qualified,
155	                "rate_range": product.get("rate_range") or {"min": 0, "max": 0},
156	                "term_range": product.get("term_months") or {"min": 0, "max": 0},
157	                "dscr": dscr,
158	            }
159	
160	        return results

1	"""
2	Context Builder — Assembles pre-processed financial data into structured
3	prompts for LLM reasoning.
4	
5	ARCHITECTURE PRINCIPLE:
6	The LLM is the reasoning layer ONLY. All financial data — credit scores,
7	bank transactions, categorization, DSCR, qualification matching — is computed
8	deterministically in the data layer and injected into the LLM prompt as
9	structured context. The LLM never fetches or computes financial data itself.
10	
11	New in this version:
12	  - build_initial_context_for_user(user_id): queries real MongoDB data via
13	    analytics services for an authenticated user (replaces company_id approach)
14	  - get_missing_data_response(context, intent): returns structured info about
15	    which data sources are missing for a given intent
16	"""
17	
18	import logging
19	from pathlib import Path
20	from typing import Any, Callable, Optional
21	
22	from agent.services.loanproducts.service import get_all_catalog_products, loan_product_to_engine_dict
23	from agent.tools.credit_bureau import CreditBureauService
24	from agent.tools.plaid_bank import PlaidBankService
25	from agent.tools.quickbooks import QuickBooksService
26	from agent.tools.qualification_engine import QualificationEngine
27	from agent.prompts.prompt_manager import prompt_manager
28	from agent.core.config import settings
29	
30	logger = logging.getLogger("loangen.context_builder")
31	
32	PROMPTS_DIR = Path(__file__).parent / "prompts"
33	
34	# ── Intent → required data sources mapping ────────────────────────────────────
35	# Which data sources must be connected for a complete answer per intent.
36	INTENT_DATA_REQUIREMENTS: dict[str, list[str]] = {
37	    "qb_financials": ["quickbooks"],
38	    "qualification": ["credit", "quickbooks"],
39	    "gap_coaching":  ["credit", "quickbooks", "bank"],
40	    "cross_sell":    ["bank", "quickbooks"],
41	    "comparison":    [],   # general knowledge, no user data required
42	    "general":       [],   # general knowledge, no user data required
43	}
44	
45	# ── Source display names and dashboard routes ─────────────────────────────────
46	SOURCE_META: dict[str, dict] = {
47	    "credit": {
48	        "label":    "Credit Report",
49	        "cta":      "Connect Credit Bureau",
50	        "route":    "/user/dashboard?connect=credit",
51	        "why":      "Your credit score and tradelines determine which loan products you qualify for and at what rates.",
52	    },
53	    "bank": {
54	        "label":    "Bank Account",
55	        "cta":      "Connect Bank Account",
56	        "route":    "/user/dashboard?connect=bank",
57	        "why":      "Bank transaction data shows your cash flow patterns, average balances, and financial stability.",
58	    },
59	    "quickbooks": {
60	        "label":    "QuickBooks",
61	        "cta":      "Connect QuickBooks",
62	        "route":    "/user/dashboard?connect=quickbooks",
63	        "why":      "QuickBooks provides your revenue, P&L, and balance sheet data needed to calculate DSCR and revenue-based qualification.",
64	    },
65	}
66	
67	
68	def _load_prompt(name: str) -> str:
69	    return (PROMPTS_DIR / name).read_text()
70	
71	
72	class ContextBuilder:
73	    """
74	    Fetches data from external services (Plaid, credit bureaus, QuickBooks)
75	    and assembles it into structured context strings for LLM prompts.
76	
77	    The LLM receives pre-processed summaries, never raw data.
78	    Data categorization (e.g., Plaid transaction categories) is done by
79	    the respective service's own models, not the LLM.
80	    """
81	
82	    def __init__(self):
83	        self.credit_service = CreditBureauService()
84	        self.plaid_service = PlaidBankService()
85	        self.qb_service = QuickBooksService()
86	        self.qualification_engine = QualificationEngine()
87	
88	    async def build_initial_context(self, company_id: str | None) -> dict[str, Any]:
89	        """
90	        Build the initial context bundle when an SMB connects.
91	        Returns a dict with all available pre-processed data.
92	        """
93	        context: dict[str, Any] = {
94	            "company_id": company_id,
95	            "has_qb": False,
96	            "has_credit": False,
97	            "has_bank": False,
98	            "company": None,
99	            "financials": None,
100	            "credit_personal": None,
101	            "credit_business": None,
102	            "bank_data": None,
103	            "readiness_score": None,
104	            "loan_products_engine": [],
105	        }
106	
107	        if not company_id:
108	            return context
109	
110	        products_doc = await get_all_catalog_products()
111	        context["loan_products_engine"] = [loan_product_to_engine_dict(p) for p in products_doc]
112	
113	        company = await self.qb_service.get_company(company_id)
114	        if company:
115	            context["has_qb"] = True
116	            context["company"] = company
117	            context["financials"] = await self.qb_service.get_financials(company_id)
118	            context["readiness_score"] = await self.qb_service.get_readiness_score(company_id)
119	
120	        credit = await self.credit_service.get_reports(company_id)
121	        if credit:
122	            context["has_credit"] = True
123	            context["credit_personal"] = credit.get("personal")
124	            context["credit_business"] = credit.get("business")
125	
126	        bank = await self.plaid_service.get_bank_data(company_id)
127	        if bank:
128	            context["has_bank"] = True
129	            context["bank_data"] = bank
130	
131	        return context
132	
133	    async def build_initial_context_for_user(
134	        self,
135	        user_id: str,
136	        db_debug_hook: Optional[Callable[[str, dict], None]] = None,
137	    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
138	        """Build context using real MongoDB data for an authenticated user.
139	
140	        This is the production replacement for build_initial_context() which
141	        used a company_id and mock services. This method queries the actual
142	        analytics services (CreditBureauAnalyticsService, PlaidAnalyticsService,
143	        QuickBooksAnalyticsService) using the user's MongoDB ObjectId.
144	
145	        Returns:
146	            Context dict with has_credit / has_bank / has_qb flags, all
147	            available financial data, and a missing_sources list.
148	        """
149	        context: dict[str, Any] = {
150	            "user_id":         user_id,
151	            "has_qb":          False,
152	            "has_credit":      False,
153	            "has_bank":        False,
154	            "company":         None,
155	            "financials":      None,
156	            "credit_personal": None,
157	            "credit_business": None,
158	            "bank_data":       None,
159	            "readiness_score": None,
160	            "missing_sources": [],   # list of source keys: "credit" | "bank" | "quickbooks"
161	        }
162	
163	        # ── 1. Credit data (Array via MongoDB analytics) ───────────────────────
164	        try:
165	            from agent.analytics.services.credit_bureau_analytics import CreditBureauAnalyticsService
166	            credit_svc = CreditBureauAnalyticsService(live=settings.data_mode == "live")
167	            credit_result = await credit_svc.get_analytics(
168	                user_id,
169	                debug_hook=db_debug_hook,
170	            )
171	            if credit_result:
172	                context["has_credit"] = True
173	                context["credit_personal"] = self._map_credit_analytics(credit_result)
174	                logger.debug(f"Credit data loaded for user={user_id}")
175	            else:
176	                context["missing_sources"].append("credit")
177	                logger.debug(f"No credit data for user={user_id}")
178	        except Exception as e:
179	            logger.warning(f"Credit analytics unavailable for user={user_id}: {e}")
180	            context["missing_sources"].append("credit")
181	
182	        # ── 2. Bank data (Plaid via MongoDB analytics) ─────────────────────────
183	        try:
184	            from agent.analytics.services.plaid_analytics import PlaidAnalyticsService
185	            bank_svc = PlaidAnalyticsService(live=settings.data_mode == "live")
186	            bank_result = await bank_svc.get_analytics(
187	                smb_id=user_id,
188	                user_id=user_id,
189	                debug_hook=db_debug_hook,
190	            )
191	            if bank_result:
192	                context["has_bank"] = True
193	                context["bank_data"] = self._map_bank_analytics(bank_result)
194	                logger.debug(f"Bank data loaded for user={user_id}")
195	            else:
196	                context["missing_sources"].append("bank")
197	                logger.debug(f"No bank data for user={user_id}")
198	        except Exception as e:
199	            logger.warning(f"Bank analytics unavailable for user={user_id}: {e}")
200	            context["missing_sources"].append("bank")
201	
202	        # ── 3. QuickBooks data (via MongoDB analytics) ─────────────────────────
203	        try:
204	            from agent.analytics.services.quickbooks_analytics import QuickBooksAnalyticsService
205	            qb_svc = QuickBooksAnalyticsService(live=settings.data_mode == "live")
206	            qb_result = await qb_svc.get_analytics(
207	                user_id,
208	                debug_hook=db_debug_hook,
209	            )
210	            if qb_result:
211	                context["has_qb"] = True
212	                context["company"]    = self._map_qb_company(qb_result)
213	                context["financials"] = self._map_qb_financials(qb_result)
214	                logger.debug(f"QuickBooks data loaded for user={user_id}")
215	            else:
216	                context["missing_sources"].append("quickbooks")
217	                logger.debug(f"No QuickBooks data for user={user_id}")
218	        except Exception as e:
219	            logger.warning(f"QB analytics unavailable for user={user_id}: {e}")
220	            context["missing_sources"].append("quickbooks")
221	
222	        logger.info(
223	            f"Context built for user={user_id} — "
224	            f"credit={context['has_credit']}  bank={context['has_bank']}  qb={context['has_qb']}  "
225	            f"missing={context['missing_sources']}"
226	        )
227	        if db_debug_hook:
228	            try:
229	                db_debug_hook(
230	                    "context_builder.context_ready",
231	                    {"user_id": user_id, "context": context},
232	                )
233	            except Exception:
234	                pass
235	        return context
236	
237	    def get_missing_data_response(
238	        self,
239	        context: dict[str, Any],
240	        intent: str,
241	    ) -> Optional[dict]:
242	        """Check whether the intent requires data sources that are not connected.
243	
244	        Returns a structured dict if required data is missing, or None if all
245	        required sources are available (meaning normal enrichment can proceed).
246	
247	        Args:
248	            context: The context dict from build_initial_context_for_user().
249	            intent: Detected intent string (qualification, gap_coaching, etc.)
250	
251	        Returns:
252	            None if no data is missing for this intent.
253	            Dict with:
254	              - "missing_sources": list of {label, cta, route, why}
255	              - "connected_sources": human-readable list of connected sources
256	              - "partial_data_available": bool
257	              - "prompt_block": rendered missing_data_prompt for LLM injection
258	        """
259	        required = INTENT_DATA_REQUIREMENTS.get(intent, [])
260	        missing_keys = context.get("missing_sources", [])
261	
262	        # Which required sources are actually missing?
263	        missing_required = [k for k in required if k in missing_keys]
264	        if not missing_required:
265	            return None   # All required data is available — proceed normally
266	
267	        # Build the response structure
268	        missing_sources = [SOURCE_META[k] for k in missing_required if k in SOURCE_META]
269	
270	        connected_keys = [k for k in ["credit", "bank", "quickbooks"] if k not in missing_keys]
271	        connected_names = [SOURCE_META[k]["label"] for k in connected_keys if k in SOURCE_META]
272	
273	        # Describe what can and can't be analysed
274	        available_analysis = self._describe_available_analysis(context, intent)
275	        unavailable_analysis = self._describe_unavailable_analysis(missing_required, intent)
276	
277	        # Render the prompt block to inject into LLM messages
278	        prompt_block = prompt_manager.get_missing_data_prompt(
279	            connected_sources=", ".join(connected_names) if connected_names else "None",
280	            missing_sources=", ".join(s["label"] for s in missing_sources),
281	            available_analysis=available_analysis,
282	            unavailable_analysis=unavailable_analysis,
283	        )
284	
285	        return {
286	            "missing_sources":       missing_sources,
287	            "connected_sources":     connected_names,
288	            "partial_data_available": len(connected_keys) > 0,
289	            "prompt_block":          prompt_block,
290	        }
291	
292	    def get_data_source_status(self, context: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, bool]:
293	        """Return a simple dict of connected status for all three sources."""
294	        return {
295	            "credit":     context.get("has_credit", False),
296	            "bank":       context.get("has_bank", False),
297	            "quickbooks": context.get("has_qb", False),
298	        }
299	
300	    # ── Analytics result mappers ───────────────────────────────────────────────
301	    # These convert the structured analytics results into the flat dict format
302	    # that build_qualification_prompt / build_gap_coaching_prompt expect.
303	
304	    def _map_credit_analytics(self, result: Any) -> dict:
305	        """Map CreditBureauAnalyticsService result to context dict format."""
306	        if hasattr(result, "model_dump"):
307	            d = result.model_dump()
308	        elif isinstance(result, dict):
309	            d = result
310	        else:
311	            return {}
312	
313	        return {
314	            "fico_score":           d.get("credit_score") or d.get("fico_score"),
315	            "bureau":               d.get("bureau", "Experian"),
316	            "pull_date":            d.get("pull_date") or d.get("report_date"),
317	            "credit_utilization":   d.get("credit_utilization") or d.get("utilization_rate"),
318	            "total_debt":           d.get("total_balance") or d.get("total_debt", 0),
319	            "tradelines":           d.get("tradelines", []),
320	            "delinquencies":        d.get("delinquent_accounts") or d.get("delinquencies", 0),
321	            "inquiries_last_12_months": d.get("hard_inquiries") or d.get("inquiries_12mo", 0),
322	            "public_records":       d.get("public_records", []),
323	        }
324	
325	    def _map_bank_analytics(self, result: Any) -> dict:
326	        """Map PlaidAnalyticsService result to context dict format."""
327	        if hasattr(result, "model_dump"):
328	            d = result.model_dump()
329	        elif isinstance(result, dict):
330	            d = result
331	        else:
332	            return {}
333	
334	        return {
335	            "accounts":       d.get("accounts", []),
336	            "last_refresh":   d.get("last_synced") or d.get("last_refresh"),
337	            "cash_flow_score": d.get("cash_flow_score"),
338	            "cash_flow_analysis": {
339	                "average_monthly_income": (
340	                    d.get("avg_monthly_inflow")
341	                    or (d.get("cash_flow_metrics") or {}).get("avg_monthly_inflow")
342	                    or d.get("average_monthly_income", 0)
343	                ),
344	                "average_monthly_expenses": (
345	                    d.get("avg_monthly_outflow")
346	                    or (d.get("cash_flow_metrics") or {}).get("avg_monthly_outflow")
347	                    or d.get("average_monthly_expenses", 0)
348	                ),
349	                "seasonality_index":        d.get("seasonality_index"),
350	                "income_volatility": (
351	                    d.get("income_volatility")
352	                    or (d.get("cash_flow_metrics") or {}).get("inflow_volatility_pct")
353	                ),
354	            },
355	            "monthly_snapshots": d.get("monthly_snapshots", []),
356	            "monthly_cash_flows": d.get("monthly_cash_flows", []),
357	            "expense_categories": d.get("expense_categories", []),
358	            "income_categories": d.get("income_categories", []),
359	            "total_transactions": d.get("total_transactions", 0),
360	            "date_range_start": d.get("date_range_start"),
361	            "date_range_end": d.get("date_range_end"),
362	            "risk_flags":        d.get("risk_flags", []),
363	        }
364	
365	    def _map_qb_company(self, result: Any) -> dict:
366	        """Map QuickBooksAnalyticsService result to company dict."""
367	        if hasattr(result, "model_dump"):
368	            d = result.model_dump()
369	        elif isinstance(result, dict):
370	            d = result
371	        else:
372	            return {}
373	
374	        company = d.get("company_profile") or d.get("company") or {}
375	        if hasattr(company, "model_dump"):
376	            company = company.model_dump()
377	        return {
378	            "company_name":    company.get("company_name") or d.get("company_name"),
379	            "industry":        company.get("industry"),
380	            "naics_code":      company.get("naics_code"),
381	            "year_established": company.get("start_date") or company.get("fiscal_year_start_month"),
382	            "employees":       company.get("employee_count"),
383	            "city":            company.get("city"),
384	            "state":           company.get("state"),
385	        }
386	
387	    def _map_qb_financials(self, result: Any) -> dict:
388	        """Map QuickBooksAnalyticsService result to financials dict.
389	
390	        QBAnalyticsResult uses income_statement.total_income (not total_revenue),
391	        balance_sheet.cash_and_bank, and top-level revenue, net_income, dscr, etc.
392	        """
393	        if hasattr(result, "model_dump"):
394	            d = result.model_dump()
395	        elif isinstance(result, dict):
396	            d = result
397	        else:
398	            return {}
399	
400	        income = d.get("income_statement") or {}
401	        balance = d.get("balance_sheet") or {}
402	        ratios = d.get("key_metrics") or {}
403	
404	        revenue = (
405	            income.get("total_income")
406	            or income.get("total_revenue")
407	            or income.get("revenue")
408	            or d.get("revenue", 0)
409	        )
410	        net_income = income.get("net_income") or d.get("net_income", 0)
411	        profit_margin = (
412	            income.get("profit_margin_pct")
413	            or income.get("profit_margin")
414	            or d.get("profit_margin", 0)
415	        )
416	        current_ratio = (
417	            balance.get("current_ratio")
418	            or ratios.get("current_ratio")
419	            or d.get("current_ratio", 0)
420	        )
421	        debt_to_equity = (
422	            balance.get("debt_to_equity")
423	            or ratios.get("debt_to_equity")
424	            or d.get("debt_to_equity", 0)
425	        )
426	        cash_on_hand = (
427	            balance.get("cash_and_bank")
428	            or balance.get("cash")
429	            or balance.get("cash_on_hand")
430	            or d.get("cash_on_hand", 0)
431	        )
432	        dscr = d.get("dscr") or ratios.get("dscr")
433	        runway_months = d.get("runway_months") or ratios.get("runway_months")
434	
435	        return {
436	            "period": income.get("period"),
437	            "revenue": revenue,
438	            "net_income": net_income,
439	            "profit_margin": profit_margin,
440	            "revenue_growth_yoy": income.get("revenue_growth_yoy") or d.get("revenue_growth_yoy", 0),
441	            "current_ratio": current_ratio,
442	            "debt_to_equity": debt_to_equity,
443	            "cash_on_hand": cash_on_hand,
444	            "monthly_burn_rate": income.get("monthly_burn_rate") or d.get("monthly_burn_rate", 0),
445	            "accounts_receivable": balance.get("accounts_receivable") or d.get("accounts_receivable", 0),
446	            "dscr": dscr,
447	            "runway_months": runway_months,
448	        }
449	
450	    def _describe_available_analysis(self, context: dict, intent: str) -> str:
451	        """Describe what can be analysed with currently connected data."""
452	        parts = []
453	        if context.get("has_credit"):
454	            parts.append("Credit score assessment, credit utilization, tradeline analysis, delinquency check")
455	        if context.get("has_bank"):
456	            parts.append("Cash flow patterns, average daily balance, income stability, NSF/overdraft check")
457	        if context.get("has_qb"):
458	            parts.append("Revenue verification, P&L analysis, DSCR calculation, balance sheet ratios")
459	        if not parts:
460	            parts.append("General loan product education and typical qualification criteria")
461	        return "; ".join(parts)
462	
463	    def _describe_unavailable_analysis(self, missing_keys: list[str], intent: str) -> str:

644	    def get_data_availability_summary(self, context: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
645	        """Summarize what data is available for this SMB."""
646	        parts = []
647	        if context.get("has_qb"):
648	            parts.append("QuickBooks financials (revenue, P&L, balance sheet)")
649	        if context.get("has_credit"):
650	            parts.append("Credit bureau reports (personal + business)")
651	        if context.get("has_bank"):
652	            parts.append("Bank feed data from Plaid (transactions, cash flow)")

659	    def _format_qualification_results(self, results: dict) -> str:
660	        """Format qualification results for prompt injection."""
661	        if not results:
662	            return "No qualification results computed"
663	
664	        lines = []
665	        for _key, result in results.items():
666	            product_name = result.get("product_name") or _key
667	            status = "QUALIFIED" if result.get("qualifies") else "NOT QUALIFIED"
668	            lines.append(f"  {product_name}: {status}")
669	            if result.get("gaps"):
670	                for gap in result["gaps"]:
671	                    lines.append(f"    - Gap: {gap}")
672	            if result.get("match_score"):
673	                lines.append(f"    Match score: {result['match_score']}%")
674	        return "\n".join(lines)
675	
676	    def _format_matched_lenders(self, lenders: list[dict]) -> str:
677	        """Format matched lenders for prompt injection."""
678	        if not lenders:
679	            return "No lenders matched"
680	
681	        lines = []
682	        for l in lenders[:10]:
683	            name = l.get("name", "Unknown")
684	            score = l.get("match_score", 0)
685	            qualifies = "YES" if l.get("qualifies") else "NO"
686	            rate = l.get("rate_range", "N/A")
687	            lines.append(f"  {name}: Match={score}% | Qualifies={qualifies} | Rate={rate}")
688	            if l.get("gaps"):
689	                for gap in l["gaps"][:3]:
690	                    lines.append(f"    - {gap}")
691	        return "\n".join(lines)
